steps.title: Set up the cost formula
steps.content:
Robert pays \$20 as an initial fee, plus \$0.70 for each mile driven. For 38 miles, the total cost is:

$$\text{Total Cost} = 20 + 0.70 \times 38$$

steps.title: Calculate the total amount
steps.content:
Multiply the per-mile rate by the number of miles:

$$0.70 \times 38 = 26.6$$

Add this to the initial fee:

$$20 + 26.6 = 46.60$$

The total amount charged is \$46.60. That matches choice A — A is correct.
